description: This neuron detects line breaks or newlines that follow the end of titles, headings, code comment blocks, or other content sections, often marking the transition to new content.
This neuron detects newline or whitespace characters that appear at the end of a line or section, often preceding a new paragraph, section break, or structural element in formatted text.
This neuron detects line breaks or newlines that occur immediately after the end of a title, heading, or labeled section.
This neuron detects newline characters or line breaks, particularly those occurring after section headers, code comments, or at the end of titles and headings.
This neuron detects newline characters or line breaks that occur at the end of a section, heading, or code comment line, often marking a transition to new content.
This neuron detects line breaks or newlines that occur after the end of a title, heading, or institutional affiliation, typically separating header content from body text.
This neuron detects newline characters or line breaks, particularly those that occur after section headers, code comments, or other structural elements in formatted text.
The neuron detects newline characters or line breaks, particularly when they appear after content or at the end of a line in code or formatted text.
This neuron detects section headers, titles, or labels that introduce topics or categorize content, particularly in formal or structured documents.
This neuron detects periods that mark the end of a complete sentence or thought, particularly when followed by a transition to a new but related topic or elaboration.
This neuron detects sentence-ending periods, particularly those that conclude complete thoughts or mark transitions between distinct sections of text.
This neuron detects sentence-ending periods that conclude introductory or contextualizing statements, particularly those that set up a topic before elaborating on it in subsequent sentences.

This neuron detects programming and document structure keywords that introduce or declare a namespace, module, or organizational unit (such as "package", "def", "Work" as a title, "Cast" as a section header, or "Pages" as a structural element).
This neuron detects sentence-ending periods that are followed by a continuation or elaboration of the same topic in the next sentence.
This neuron detects sentence-ending periods that conclude a standalone statement or introduce a transition to a new, related topic in formal or academic writing.
This neuron detects capitalized words that appear to be titles, headings, or proper nouns at the beginning of phrases or sentences.
This neuron detects periods that end sentences, particularly those followed by continuation or expansion of the topic in the next sentence.
This neuron detects section headers, labels, or categorical terms that introduce or classify the content that follows, often appearing at the beginning of titles, abstracts, or topic headings.
This neuron detects sentence-ending periods, particularly those that conclude declarative statements or complete thoughts before a transition to new information.
This neuron detects sentence-ending periods that conclude formal or technical statements, particularly in academic, medical, or professional contexts.
This neuron detects capitalized words that serve as titles, headings, or labels at the beginning of articles, blog posts, or academic papers.
This neuron detects capitalized words at the beginning of titles, headings, proper nouns, or sentences, particularly when they mark the start of a distinct section or labeled entity.
This neuron detects words or labels that appear at the beginning of titles, headings, navigation elements, or metadata tags in web content or publications.
This neuron detects sentence-ending periods, particularly those that conclude complete statements before a new sentence or topic begins.
This neuron detects line breaks or newlines that occur at natural transition points in text, particularly after standalone dates, headings, or introductory statements and before new content begins.
This neuron detects capitalized words that begin proper nouns, typically organizational names, book/show titles, or institutions.

This neuron detects tokens that represent section headers, labels, or categorical prefixes in formatted text, particularly those marking the beginning of distinct sections or metadata fields in documents.
This neuron detects periods that mark the end of a complete sentence or thought, particularly when followed by a transition to new information or a contrasting/elaborating statement.
This neuron detects the start of a new line or paragraph break, particularly when transitioning between distinct sections or starting fresh content after a previous context.
This neuron appears to detect partial words or name fragments at the beginning of proper nouns, titles, or significant terms where text has been truncated or split.
